# Chapter 30 Reports

Machine Accounting includes a comprehensive set of reports organized into the following categories:

- Audit
- Bonus
- Cashless
- Dashboard
- GCB
- Machine Events
- Mag Card
- Slot Operation
- System Maintenance

Multiple reports can be generated and viewed simultaneously.

## 30.1 Selecting Reports

Reports are organized in a category tree. Select a category and then the specific report within that category.

## 30.2 Machine Filter Options

Three modes are available for filtering machines included in a report:

| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Display All Machines | Include all machines in the report |
| Use Filter | Apply filter criteria to select a subset of machines |
| Use Machine List | Specify individual machine numbers (comma-separated, no spaces) |

### Available Filters

When using the filter mode, the following criteria are available:

- Denomination
- Section
- Bank
- Manufacturer
- Other property-specific criteria

A preview function displays the filtered machine list with the following columns:

- Machine Number
- Denomination
- Description
- Location
- Manufacturer

## 30.3 Date Range

Reports require a date range:

- **Start Date** — Beginning of the reporting period
- **End Date** — End of the reporting period

Additional date-filter options vary by report type.

## 30.4 Report Type

Some reports support multiple output types selectable from a report type list.

## 30.5 Generating Reports

After configuring filters, date range, and report type, the report is generated. Reports display data in tabular format with subtotals and groupings.

### Example: Actual Slot Win Report

Key columns include:

- Denomination
- Machine Number
- Location
- Description
- Days on Floor
- Metered Coin In
- Legacy Coin Out Actual
- Bill Drop Actual
- Coin Drop
- Actual Voucher Drop
- Total Drop Non-Deductible
- Bonus Adjusted
- Total Drop
- Actual Fills

Data is typically broken into Non-MDMG (non-multi-denomination multi-game) and MDMG sections with subtotals.

## 30.6 Preliminary vs. Final Reports

- **Preliminary reports** — Available at any time during the period
- **Final reports** — Available only after the period is closed
